HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS GOVERNOR GAVIN NEWSOM, AND CALIFORNIA'S OFFICE OF GOVERNOR GAVIN NEWSOM's Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff's Case With Prejudice.

Off Calendar Nov-14-2022 Continued To Feb-08-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-20-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Lydia Ko, Esq. (510.879.0004; lydia.ko@doj.ca.gov) Deputy Attorney General for Defendants.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS GOVERNOR GAVIN NEWSOM and CALIFORNIA'S OFFICE OF GOVERNOR GAVIN NEWSOM'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend.

The complaint fails to state a cause of action against Governor Newsom or anyone else. Each cause of action pled must specifically state: its number, its nature and the party or parties to whom it is directed. (California Rule of Court 2.112.) The complaint here does not meet even these most basic requirements.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint And Vacate August 18, 2022 Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-03-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Julia Powell Keller-McIntyre, 415-587-8554, jkmci@earthlink.net, Pro Per Plaintiff,
Lydia V. Ko., Esq., Deputy Attorney General State of California, (510) 879-0004, Lydia.Ko@doj.ca.gov, Attorneys for Specially Appearing Defendant Governor Gavin Newsom and California 's Office of Governer Gavin Newsom.

Defendant's counsel was not properly notified that plaintiff was contesting today's tentative ruling.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows::

DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA'S OFFICE OF GOVER GAVIN NEWSOM, GAVIN NEWSOM'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint And Vacate August 18, 2022 Prove-Up Hearing

The motion to quash is granted. No timely opposition filed.

Counsel for defendant states that she will accepet service of summons and complaint on behalf of defendant's California's Office of Governer Gavin Newsom and Gavin Newsom and file an Acknowledgment of Receipt of Service.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
